human_actions_pending.context: Three options offered at pause: (a) full auto Wave 1->Wave 3 chain; (b) Wave 1 only with human review of FINDINGS.md before Wave 2; (c) review plans/UI-SPEC/RESEARCH first then resume manually. Recommended (b) because the critique sweep is novel and FINDINGS.md is the natural human checkpoint.

decisions.decision: /impeccable v3.0.1 API: per-context `/impeccable critique <URL>` with browser state set first (theme via localStorage 'nicu_assistant_theme'; mode via SegmentedToggle click; viewport via Playwright). NO --theme/--viewport/--mode flags.
decisions.rationale: Researcher verified by reading SKILL.md + reference/critique.md + live-probing CLI

decisions.decision: CLI scan form is `npx --yes -p impeccable@latest impeccable detect --json <files>` — NOT the `npx impeccable --json` form initially in UI-SPEC
decisions.rationale: UI-SPEC researcher had wrong form; phase researcher corrected; planner uses corrected form in 04-01-PLAN.md Task 2 STEP D

decisions.decision: Author PRODUCT.md at repo root manually from CLAUDE.md §Design Context (NOT interactive /impeccable teach in --auto mode). Amends D-08a to add PRODUCT.md as 3rd named exception to the PERT-route allowlist.
decisions.rationale: PRODUCT.md is required by /impeccable teach per SKILL.md §Setup; without it critique degrades to generic output. Interactive teach blocks in --auto.

decisions.decision: Per-pass parallelism: SEQUENTIAL (1 dev server, 8 critique passes one after another)
decisions.rationale: Researcher recommendation; safer for shared dev server + per-context browser state isolation

decisions.decision: Wave 3 re-runs all 8 critique passes on post-fix UI
decisions.rationale: Per CONTEXT D-04 score-acceptance contract: aggregate + per-context score must be measured against the post-fix UI, not the pre-fix critique transcripts

decisions.decision: Wave 2 is a PLACEHOLDER plan with `placeholder: true` per CONTEXT D-07a replan beat
decisions.rationale: Fix-plan scope is unknown until Wave 1 ships findings; orchestrator runs /gsd-plan-phase 4 --gaps after Wave 1 to author actual fix plans

decisions.decision: Clinical-gate counts inherit verbatim from Phase 3.1 closure
decisions.rationale: Phase 4 makes no test/logic changes; vitest 425/425, pert.spec 12/12, full Playwright 117/118 with disclaimer-banner.spec.ts:28 baseline flake all expected to hold. If they shift, D-08a allows e2e selector updates in same commit as P1 fix.

next_action: Choose execution mode (a/b/c offered at pause) then run /gsd-execute-phase 4 --auto --ws pert (or --wave 1 for option b)
context_notes: Phase 4 is the v1.15 release-readiness design-polish sweep. CONTEXT/UI-SPEC/RESEARCH/Plans all committed and verified. Ready to execute. Recommended pause point is post-Wave-1 (after 04-FINDINGS.md ships) to let user eyeball the triage before Wave 2 fix plans are authored. Phase 3.1 (the prior phase) closed cleanly with PERT-TEST-05 FULL + KI-1 RESOLVED at commit f2da16d.

advisory_constraints.name: Workstream-aware SDK calls return phase_found:false
advisory_constraints.description: The gsd-sdk init.phase-op / init.plan-phase / init.execute-phase commands look in .planning/phases/, NOT .planning/workstreams/pert/phases/. Workflows that depend on these calls (e.g. STATE.md auto-recording via state.begin-phase / state.planned-phase) silently no-op for workstream-scoped phases. Orchestrator must work around by directly editing STATE.md + ROADMAP.md + REQUIREMENTS.md with the workstream-local paths.
advisory_constraints.mitigation: When invoking gsd-execute-phase or gsd-plan-phase orchestrators, always pass --ws pert AND verify the phase directory exists at .planning/workstreams/pert/phases/04-design-polish-impeccable/. Fall back to direct file edits + manual STATE.md updates if SDK queries return phase_found:false.
